$comment: D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ASSIGN-VALUE-POSITION end-to-end: a bit-field mutation is a read-modify-write of the allocation unit. It was correct ONLY for statement plain-`=`; every other form took a full-unit store (clobbering the packed neighbour + skipping truncation) and value forms yielded the un-truncated value. This drives value-position `=`/`+=`/`++`/`--` AND statement `+=`/`++`/`--`, asserting value-yield truncation semantics (`(g.a=300)`==12, `(g.a+=7)`==3, post-`++` OLD, pre-`++` NEW), neighbour preservation (g.b stays 5), a NON-bit-field member through the SAME reconstruction path (g.plain, the blast-radius guard), and single evaluation of a `ga[i++].a += 6` base (i ends 1). acc(23)+g.a(14)+g.b(5)-ga[1].a(0) = 42; any miscompile returns a distinct non-42 code. LAYOUT-RULE-AGNOSTIC (write+read share the packing). The optimizedPipelines arm proves the flow survives Mem2Reg/ConstFold; the baseline arm is the real-codegen memory witness. arm64 runs under qemu; macho on the macos-latest leg.
